lessons.title: Les bases de HTML
lessons.content:
HTML (HyperText Markup Language) est le langage de balisage standard pour créer des pages web. Il décrit la structure d'une page web et consiste en une série d'éléments. Les éléments HTML indiquent au navigateur comment afficher le contenu. ## Structure de base Un document HTML commence toujours par `` suivi de la balise ``. À l'intérieur on trouve `` pour les métadonnées et `` pour le contenu visible. ## Les balises essentielles - `

` à `

` : Titres - `

` : Paragraphes - `` : Liens hypertexte - `` : Images - `

` et `` : Conteneurs

lessons.title: Introduction au CSS
lessons.content:
CSS (Cascading Style Sheets) est utilisé pour styliser les éléments HTML. Il permet de contrôler la couleur, la police, l'espacement, la mise en page et bien plus encore.

## Sélecteurs CSS

- **Sélecteur d'élément** : `p { color: red; }`
- **Sélecteur de classe** : `.ma-classe { font-size: 16px; }`
- **Sélecteur d'identifiant** : `#mon-id { background: blue; }`

## Le modèle de boîte

Chaque élément HTML est une boîte avec margin, border, padding et content.

lessons.title: Python : Fondamentaux
lessons.content:
Python est un langage de programmation interprété, de haut niveau et à usage général. Sa philosophie de conception met l'accent sur la lisibilité du code.

## Variables et types

```python
nom = 'Alice'      # str
age = 25           # int
prix = 19.99       # float
actif = True       # bool
```

## Structures de contrôle

```python
if age >= 18:
    print('Majeur')
else:
    print('Mineur')

for i in range(5):
    print(i)
```

questions.question: What does HTML stand for?
Choose the correct HTML element for the largest heading:
Which character is used to indicate an end tag?
What is the correct way to create a hyperlink?
Which tag is used to insert an image?
What is the correct HTML element for inserting a line break?
Which attribute is used to define inline styles?
What does the <meta> tag define?
Which tag is used to define the title of an HTML document?
What is the correct HTML element for the most important heading?
What does CSS stand for?
Which property is used to change the background color?
How do you add a comment in CSS?
Which property is used to change the text color?
Which CSS property controls the text size?
How do you select an element with id 'header'?
Which property is used to add space inside an element?
What is the default value of the position property?
How do you create a function in Python?
What is the correct way to create a list in Python?
Which of the following is NOT a data type in Python?
What does the len() function do in Python?
options: Returns the length of an object
questions.answer: Returns the length of an object

questions.question: How do you import a module in Python?
How do you create a dictionary in Python?
What is the correct way to create a loop in Python?
Which keyword declares a block-scoped variable in JavaScript?
What does `typeof null` return in JavaScript?
How do you write a comment in JavaScript?
Which method adds an element to the end of an array?
What is the purpose of the 'srcset' attribute in an tag?
options: To define multiple image sources for different screen sizes
To set the source of the image
To add alt text for accessibility
To define a CSS class for the image
questions.answer: To define multiple image sources for different screen sizes

questions.question: Which HTML5 element is used to draw graphics via scripting (usually JavaScript)?
What is the difference between <section> and <div> in HTML5?
options: <section> is a semantic element representing a thematic grouping; <div> is a generic container
questions.answer: <section> is a semantic element representing a thematic grouping; <div> is a generic container

questions.question: Which attribute makes a form field required before submission?
What is the correct way to embed a video in HTML5?
What does the 'defer' attribute do on a <script> tag?
options: Loads the script asynchronously and executes it after HTML parsing
Loads the script before parsing HTML
questions.answer: Loads the script asynchronously and executes it after HTML parsing

questions.question: Which HTML element is used to define navigation links?
What is the purpose of the <datalist> element in HTML5?
options: Provides autocomplete suggestions for an <input> element
Displays a list of data in a table
questions.answer: Provides autocomplete suggestions for an <input> element

questions.question: What does the 'aria-label' attribute do?
options: Provides an accessible name for an element for screen readers
Sets the language of an element
questions.answer: Provides an accessible name for an element for screen readers

questions.question: Which input type would you use to let users pick a date?
How do you make text bold in CSS?
Which CSS property is used to set the space between elements (outside the border)?
Which CSS property creates a flexible container for Flexbox layout?
What does 'z-index' control in CSS?
options: The stacking order of overlapping elements
questions.answer: The stacking order of overlapping elements

questions.question: What is the difference between 'relative' and 'absolute' positioning in CSS?
options: 'relative' offsets from its normal position; 'absolute' is positioned relative to its nearest positioned ancestor
'absolute' offsets from its normal position; 'relative' is positioned relative to the viewport
They both position relative to the parent element
questions.answer: 'relative' offsets from its normal position; 'absolute' is positioned relative to its nearest positioned ancestor

questions.question: Which CSS property is used to make a website responsive by changing styles based on screen size?
What does 'box-sizing: border-box' do?
options: Makes padding and border included in the element's total width and height
Removes the border from the element
Adds extra space around the element
Resets all box model properties to default
questions.answer: Makes padding and border included in the element's total width and height

questions.question: Which CSS property aligns flex items along the cross axis?
What is a CSS pseudo-class?
options: A keyword added to a selector that specifies a special state of the element
A selector that targets an element's child
questions.answer: A keyword added to a selector that specifies a special state of the element

questions.question: What does CSS specificity determine?
options: Which CSS rule is applied when multiple rules target the same element
How fast a CSS rule is applied
The order in which CSS files are loaded
The scope of a CSS variable
questions.answer: Which CSS rule is applied when multiple rules target the same element

questions.question: Which CSS function is used to apply a smooth color transition between two or more colors?
What does the CSS property 'overflow: hidden' do?
options: Clips content that exceeds the element's bounds and hides the scrollbar
Adds a scrollbar when content overflows
Collapses the element when it overflows
Makes the element invisible when overflowed
questions.answer: Clips content that exceeds the element's bounds and hides the scrollbar

questions.question: Which method removes the last element of an array in JavaScript?
How do you declare a constant in JavaScript?
Which operator is used for strict equality in JavaScript?
How do you write 'Hello World' to the browser console?
What keyword is used to define a function in JavaScript?
How do you check the length of a string in JavaScript?
What is a closure in JavaScript?
options: A function that has access to its outer function's scope even after the outer function has returned
A way to close the browser window using JavaScript
A method to end a loop
A function with no return value
questions.answer: A function that has access to its outer function's scope even after the outer function has returned

questions.question: What is the difference between '==' and '===' in JavaScript?
options: '==' checks value only; '===' checks both value and type
'===' checks value only; '==' checks both value and type
questions.answer: '==' checks value only; '===' checks both value and type

questions.question: What does the 'this' keyword refer to inside an arrow function?
options: The enclosing lexical context (not the function itself)
The DOM element the function is attached to
questions.answer: The enclosing lexical context (not the function itself)

questions.question: What does the Array.map() method do?
options: Creates a new array by applying a function to each element
Filters elements from an array based on a condition
Reduces an array to a single value
Finds the index of an element in an array
questions.answer: Creates a new array by applying a function to each element

questions.question: What is event bubbling in JavaScript?
options: An event propagates from the target element up to the root of the DOM
An event fires multiple times on the same element
An event is cancelled before reaching the target
An event is created dynamically using JavaScript
questions.answer: An event propagates from the target element up to the root of the DOM

questions.question: What is the purpose of 'async/await' in JavaScript?
options: To write asynchronous code that reads like synchronous code
To block the main thread until a promise resolves
questions.answer: To write asynchronous code that reads like synchronous code

questions.question: What does JSON.stringify() do?
options: Converts a JavaScript object to a JSON string
Parses a JSON string into a JavaScript object
Validates whether a string is valid JSON
questions.answer: Converts a JavaScript object to a JSON string

questions.question: What is the difference between 'let' and 'var' in JavaScript?
options: 'let' is block-scoped; 'var' is function-scoped and hoisted
They are identical in modern JavaScript
questions.answer: 'let' is block-scoped; 'var' is function-scoped and hoisted

questions.question: What does the spread operator (...) do in JavaScript?
options: Expands an iterable into individual elements
Creates a new scope for variables
Converts an array into a string
questions.answer: Expands an iterable into individual elements

questions.question: Which method is used to attach an event listener to a DOM element?
What is the output of print(2 ** 3) in Python?
How do you convert a string to an integer in Python?
Which keyword is used to handle exceptions in Python?
What is the Big O time complexity of Binary Search?
Which data structure uses LIFO (Last In, First Out) order?
What is the time complexity of accessing an element in a Python dictionary?
What is a Python list comprehension?
options: A concise way to create a list by applying an expression to each item in an iterable
A method to compress a list
A way to sort a list in-place
A function that filters a list
questions.answer: A concise way to create a list by applying an expression to each item in an iterable

questions.question: What is the difference between a list and a tuple in Python?
options: Lists are mutable; tuples are immutable
Tuples are mutable; lists are immutable
Lists can only hold integers; tuples can hold any type
questions.answer: Lists are mutable; tuples are immutable

questions.question: What does the *args parameter do in a Python function?
options: Allows a function to accept any number of positional arguments
Multiplies all arguments passed to the function
Restricts the function to a single argument
questions.answer: Allows a function to accept any number of positional arguments

questions.question: What is a Python decorator?
options: A function that wraps another function to extend its behavior
A way to add comments to code
A design pattern for creating objects
A method to format string output
questions.answer: A function that wraps another function to extend its behavior

questions.question: What is the time complexity of Bubble Sort in the worst case?
Which Python built-in function returns the largest item in an iterable?
What does the 'yield' keyword do in a Python function?
options: Turns the function into a generator that returns values one at a time
Returns a value and terminates the function
questions.answer: Turns the function into a generator that returns values one at a time
